_comment: Phase 2 follow-on — N-way, SELF-OPTIMIZING, HINT-AWARE cross-host streaming.
A single 6-stage StreamPipeline with `segments: 3` (no explicit cut_after): the
partitioner auto-picks the 2 CHEAPEST stage boundaries to cut on, using per-stage
(relative data emitted; lower = cheaper to ship). It then chains M StreamPipeline
segments across M machines by embedded RDMA and returns each round to node 0
(StreamOutput sink). `segments` clamps to the online node count, so this runs as
2 segments on a 2-node cluster and 3 on a 3-node cluster automatically.
Here img_load_ppm (prefer) and the grayscale→equalize boundary (low out_weight)
are the cheap cuts; img_blur is marked avoid. Output is byte-identical to the
Submit via `node-agent submit` (coordinator partitions + RDMA-stages + runs).
